reasoning: When lo == hi, `between(lo, ki, hi)` requires `ki.lt_spec(hi)` and `!ki.lt_spec(lo)` simultaneously — since lo == hi, no `ki` satisfies both, making the universal quantifier vacuously true. This is standard behavior for empty intervals in any range-based predicate.

An empty range being consistent with any endpoint is the expected semantics of vacuous truth over an empty domain. This is identical to "for all x in ∅, P(x)" being true for any P — not a spec weakness.

This is a positive sanity check confirming `range_consistent` is not trivially true for non-empty intervals. If a key strictly between lo and hi maps to a different endpoint, range_consistent correctly returns false. This is the expected and desirable behavior.
